title: Parity: announce (Rust/Zephyr/Python)
description: Policy: three-impl parity. Module: announce. Rust: partial (247 LoC codec only in lichen-core). Zephyr: complete (495 LoC). Python: complete (1141 LoC). Gap: Rust has only message codec. Missing processor (gradient tracking) and scheduler (periodic TX) that Zephyr/Python have.
close_reason:
Verified parity gaps. Filed 3 specific sub-beads:
- qkve.1: Rust announce processor (gradient tracking, sig verification)
- qkve.2: Rust announce scheduler (periodic TX)
- qkve.3: Zephyr announce scheduler (periodic TX - was incorrectly marked complete)

Python is the only complete implementation with all 4 components: messages (223 LoC), processor (305 LoC), scheduler (303 LoC), coords (273 LoC). Zephyr has RX path but no TX scheduler. Rust has codec only.
